items.prompt: In Snorkel-style programmatic labeling, what do labeling functions provide?
choices: Weak, possibly conflicting votes from rules or noisy models over subsets of data
Perfect gold labels for every row with no noise
Only storage paths for unlabeled files
A single deterministic label with no aggregation
items.explain: Section 11.6 and Example 11.14 describe noisy labeling functions combined probabilistically.

items.prompt: Example 11.14 requires a full hand-labeled email corpus before any training labels exist.
items.explain: Snorkel outputs probabilistic training labels without requiring a full hand-labeled corpus.

items.prompt: What does data programming typically emit instead of hard labels?
choices: Probabilistic labels reflecting source reliability and uncertainty
Only empty labels for every instance
items.explain: Data programming learns labeling-function quality and combines votes into soft labels.

items.prompt: Name one benefit and one limit of heuristic-based labeling.
items.rubric: Benefit: scale/domain knowledge/efficiency. Limit: noise/sparse coverage/edge cases.
items.sample_answer: Heuristics scale domain rules quickly, but they are noisy and miss edge cases.

items.prompt: Snorkel can estimate labeling-function accuracies and reconcile conflicting weak labels.
items.explain: The generative model / EM step adjusts weights and infers likely true labels.
